summary: Russia launched two Oreshnik medium‑range ballistic missiles, one striking the Avdiivka area, while Ukrainian forces reported shooting down 246 hostile UAVs amid a large‑scale Shahed attack. Russian intelligence warned of a possible massive rocket strike on Kyiv and Kyiv region within three days, listing several cruise and hypersonic missile types. Civilian casualties continued with aerial bombings in Kramatorsk and Druzhkivka and multiple UAV incursions across the front.
key_points: Two Oreshnik missiles were used by Russia, one hitting the Avdiivka area (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).
Ukraine’s air defence downed 246 enemy UAVs during a massive Shahed‑type attack involving 262 strike drones from multiple directions (Source: @kpszsu/62715).

Russian warnings indicate a high threat of a mass rocket strike on Kyiv and Kyiv region within three days, citing possible use of X‑22/32, X‑101, Iskander‑M, Zircon, Kalibr, Kinzhal and strike UAVs (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
Shahed‑136 UAV launches were noted from the Primorsko‑Akhtarsk area (Source: @eRadarrua/93637).
Nine Shahed drones flew from Chernihiv toward Vyshhorod district in Kyiv region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
At least five civilians were injured after two aerial bombs hit Kramatorsk city centre, damaging multi‑storey buildings and vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
A Russian FAB strike on a multi‑storey building in Druzhkivka killed a 70‑year‑old woman and injured four other women (Source: @UkraineNow/81440).
UAV groups from east Kharkiv moved toward Izium and Balakliia, and a UAV from the Black Sea headed toward Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/62738; @kpszsu/62742).

sections.executive_summary: Two Russian Oreshnik missiles struck, including one in the Avdiivka area. Ukraine’s air defence downed 246 hostile UAVs amid a large Shahed drone barrage. Russian warnings of a possible mass rocket strike on Kyiv heighten strategic tension.

points: Ukrainian air defence forces reported shooting down 246 enemy UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Italmas types, during an attack that involved 262 strike drones from directions such as Orël, Kursk, and Shatalovo, with ten strike UAV impacts recorded across nine locations (Source: @kpszsu/62715).

Civilian casualties continued as Russian aerial bombs struck Kramatorsk city centre, injuring at least five people and damaging multiple multi‑storey buildings and vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
In Druzhkivka, a Russian FAB strike on a multi‑storey building killed a 70‑year‑old woman and caused mine‑explosive injuries to four other women aged 40‑73 (Source: @UkraineNow/81440).
Ukrainian forces observed a group of UAVs on the east side of Kharkiv moving toward Izium and Balakliia, indicating continued drone pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/62738).
A UAV launched from the Black Sea was tracked heading toward the Odesa region, suggesting maritime‑origin drone activity on the southern flank (Source: @kpszsu/62742).
Russia employed two Oreshnik medium‑range ballistic missiles, with the second missile striking the temporarily Russian‑controlled area near Avdiivka (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).

Russian intelligence issued a high‑threat warning of a possible mass rocket strike on Kyiv and Kyiv region within the next three days, listing potential weapons such as X‑22/32, X‑101, Iskander‑M, Zircon, Kalibr, Kinzhal and a moderate number of strike UAVs (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
Shahed‑136 UAV launches were reported from the Primorsko‑Akhtarsk region, indicating preparation for further drone attacks (Source: @eRadarrua/93637).

Nine Shahed drones were launched from Chernihiv heading toward the Vyshhorod district of Kyiv region, demonstrating continued pressure on the northern approaches to the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
A fast missile was observed over Zaporizhzhia moving toward Dnipropetrovsk, suggesting missile strikes aimed at critical infrastructure in the southern‑central axis (Source: @kpszsu/62761).
UAV groups from east Kharkiv advanced toward Izium and Balakliia, targeting key logistics hubs on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/62738).

Shahed‑type UAV attacks originated from multiple directions including Orël, Kursk, and Shatalovo, impacting eastern sectors and resulting in ten strike UAV hits across nine locations (Source: @kpszsu/62715).
A reactive missile was reported near Romny in Sumy region, moving from Glukhov past Esman, indicating missile activity in the northeastern corridor (Source: @eRadarrua/93642).
UAV movements were noted from Chernihiv toward Kyiv region, potentially affecting the northern flank of the eastern front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
Shahed drones launched from Chernihiv targeted the Vyshhorod district of Kyiv region, posing a direct threat to the capital’s northern approaches (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
UAV activity was reported in Chernihiv and Sumy regions, with missiles moving toward Romny and Glukhov, indicating pressure on northern supply lines (Source: @eRadarrua/93642).
Russian warnings of a possible mass rocket strike on Kyiv underscore heightened threat levels for the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
UAVs were observed moving from the Black Sea toward Odesa, potentially threatening northern coastal installations (Source: @kpszsu/62742).
A fast missile over Zaporizhzhia heading to Dnipropetrovsk suggests Russian strike intent on southern‑central infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/62761).

UAV launch direction from Dnipropetrovsk region indicated strike UAVs moving toward Pokrov, while Orl‑10 drones headed to Tavriyske, and strike UAVs targeted Rivnopillya in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @eRadarrua/93639).
Shahed‑136 UAVs were noted launching from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, a southern Russian region, highlighting southern launch bases (Source: @eRadarrua/93637).
UAV from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa region points to maritime‑origin threats on the southern coastline (Source: @kpszsu/62742).
Ukraine’s air defence downed 246 hostile UAVs during a large‑scale Shahed attack, with 262 strike drones launched from multiple Russian directions (Source: @kpszsu/62715).

Russian warnings list a variety of air‑launched weapons—X‑22/32, X‑101, Iskander‑M, Zircon, Kalibr, Kinzhal—potentially targeting Kyiv, indicating an escalation in air strike capabilities (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).

Shahed‑136 UAV launches from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and nine Shahed drones from Chernihiv toward Vyshhorod demonstrate continued Russian UAV pressure on Ukrainian airspace (Source: @eRadarrua/93637; @mon1tor_ua/67218).
Aerial bombs were dropped on Kramatorsk city centre, causing civilian injuries and damage to multi‑storey structures (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
A Russian FAB strike on a Druzhkivka building resulted in civilian fatalities, highlighting the use of air‑delivered munitions against urban targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81440).

key_developments: Two Oreshnik medium‑range ballistic missiles were launched by Russia, with one striking the Avdiivka area (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).
Ukraine’s air defence reported shooting down 246 enemy UAVs during a massive Shahed‑type attack involving 262 strike drones from multiple directions (Source: @kpszsu/62715).

Russian intelligence warned of a high threat of a mass rocket strike on Kyiv and Kyiv region within three days, citing possible use of X‑22/32, X‑101, Iskander‑M, Zircon, Kalibr, Kinzhal and strike UAVs (Source: @eRadarrua/93625).
Shahed‑136 UAV launches were observed from the Primorsko‑Akhtarsk region, indicating preparation for further drone attacks (Source: @eRadarrua/93637).
Nine Shahed drones were launched from Chernihiv toward the Vyshhorod district of Kyiv region, increasing pressure on the capital’s northern approaches (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
Two aerial bombs hit Kramatorsk city centre, injuring at least five civilians and damaging multi‑storey buildings and vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/81457).
A Russian FAB strike on a multi‑storey building in Druzhkivka killed a 70‑year‑old woman and injured four other women (Source: @UkraineNow/81440).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile and UAV activity remains intense across all fronts, with medium‑range ballistic missiles, Shahed drones, and potential mass rocket launches targeting Kyiv and key regional centers. Ukrainian air defences are actively intercepting large numbers of hostile UAVs, but civilian infrastructure continues to suffer from aerial bombings and FAB strikes. The announced possibility of deploying advanced cruise and hypersonic missiles against the capital raises the risk of a significant escalation in the near term.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian forces have intensified air defence operations, successfully downing hundreds of enemy drones and reporting rapid missile interceptions on the eastern and southern axes. Local authorities in affected cities such as Kramatorsk and Druzhkivka are conducting emergency response and casualty management. International partners are monitoring the heightened threat to Kyiv, with diplomatic advisories issued for foreign nationals in the capital.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring confirms multiple launch points for Shahed drones, including Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and Chernihiv, suggesting coordinated launch corridors. The list of potential missile types for a Kyiv strike indicates Russian preparation to employ a broad spectrum of stand‑off weapons. Continued UAV incursions from the Black Sea toward Odesa highlight maritime launch capabilities that may complicate coastal defence planning.
